input.initialPrompt:
I have a clean macOS machine and want to start developing .NET MAUI apps
for iOS and Android. I can't run commands right now, but I need a complete
setup plan. Tell me exactly what I need to install, how to discover the
right versions, and how to verify everything works. Please provide the
official recommended installation sources.

data.content:
Here's a complete setup plan for .NET MAUI development on macOS:

---

## 1. Install .NET SDK

- **Source:** [https://dot.net/download](https://dot.net/download)
- Download the latest **.NET 8 SDK** (or .NET 9 if you want the newest) for macOS (pick Apple Silicon or Intel based on your Mac).
- Verify: `dotnet --version`

## 2. Install Xcode (for iOS)

- **Source:** Mac App Store or [https://developer.apple.com/xcode/](https://developer.apple.com/xcode/)
- Install the **latest stable Xcode**. This includes the iOS SDK, simulators, and build tools.
- After install, accept the license and install CLI tools:
  ```bash
  sudo xcodebuild -license accept
  xcode-select --install
  ```
- Verify: `xcodebuild -version`

## 3. Install the .NET MAUI Workload

- This pulls in all MAUI-specific SDKs and templates:
  ```bash
  dotnet workload install maui
  ```
- Discover available workloads: `dotnet workload search`
- Check what's installed: `dotnet workload list`

## 4. Install JDK (for Android)

- The MAUI workload installs a **Microsoft OpenJDK** automatically. If you need to install manually:
- **Source:** [https://learn.microsoft.com/java/openjdk/download](https://learn.microsoft.com/java/openjdk/download)
- Install **Microsoft OpenJDK 17**.
- Verify: `java -version`

## 5. Install Android SDK & Emulator

- The MAUI workload auto-provisions the Android SDK. If you need manual control:
- **Source:** [https://developer.android.com/studio](https://developer.android.com/studio) — install **Android Studio** for the SDK Manager and emulator images.
- Required components (via SDK Manager):
  - **Android SDK Platform** for your target API level (e.g., API 34)
  - **Android SDK Build-Tools** (latest)
  - **Android SDK Command-line Tools**
  - **Android Emulator** + a system image
- Set `ANDROID_HOME`:
  ```bash
  export ANDROID_HOME="$HOME/Library/Android/sdk"
  ```
- Verify: `$ANDROID_HOME/cmdline-tools/latest/bin/sdkmanager --list`

## 6. (Optional) Install an IDE

| IDE | Source |
|---|---|
| **Visual Studio Code** + C# Dev Kit extension | [https://code.visualstudio.com](https://code.visualstudio.com) |
| **JetBrains Rider** | [https://www.jetbrains.com/rider/](https://www.jetbrains.com/rider/) |

> **Note:** Visual Studio for Mac has been retired. VS Code with the **C# Dev Kit** and **.NET MAUI** extensions is the official Microsoft path on macOS now.

## 7. Discover the Right Versions

```bash
# See which .NET SDKs are installed
dotnet --list-sdks

# See installed MAUI workload versions
dotnet workload list

# See available project templates
dotnet new list maui

# Check Android SDK components
$ANDROID_HOME/cmdline-tools/latest/bin/sdkmanager --list --include_obsolete
```

## 8. Verify Everything End-to-End

```bash
# 1. Create a new MAUI app
dotnet new maui -n HelloMaui
cd HelloMaui

# 2. Build for all targets
dotnet build

# 3. Run on iOS simulator
dotnet build -t:Run -f net8.0-ios

# 4. Run on Android emulator (start emulator first)
$ANDROID_HOME/emulator/emulator -avd Pixel_5_API_34 &
dotnet build -t:Run -f net8.0-android
```

Replace `net8.0` with `net9.0` if using .NET 9.

## Quick Checklist

| ✅ | Component | How to verify |
|---|---|---|
| ☐ | .NET 8/9 SDK | `dotnet --version` |
| ☐ | MAUI workload | `dotnet workload list` |
| ☐ | Xcode + CLI tools | `xcodebuild -version` |
| ☐ | JDK 17 | `java -version` |
| ☐ | Android SDK | `$ANDROID_HOME/cmdline-tools/latest/bin/sdkmanager --version` |
| ☐ | Emulator / Simulator | Create & run an AVD / launch iOS Simulator |
| ☐ | IDE | Open project, verify IntelliSense works |
| ☐ | Build & run | `dotnet build` succeeds for both frameworks |
